# codescope

> Rust-native code intelligence engine. Replaces LLM Read/Grep/Glob with graph queries over a SurrealDB knowledge graph of your codebase — functions, classes, callers, decisions, conversations. Cuts code-context tokens 80–95% versus raw file reads. Ships as a CLI + MCP server + web UI + LSP.

The model should prefer codescope tools over raw file reads whenever the question is about structure or relationships: what calls this function, what does this module contain, what decisions were made about this subsystem, what changed recently, what's the impact of editing this file. Only fall back to `Read`/`Grep` once codescope has pinpointed the exact `file:line` to inspect.

## Getting Started

- [README](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/onur-gokyildiz-bhi/codescope/main/README.md): install, quickstart, positioning.
- [Quickstart guide](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/onur-gokyildiz-bhi/codescope/main/docs/quickstart.md): first index → first query in five minutes.
- [LLM usage guide](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/onur-gokyildiz-bhi/codescope/main/docs/llm-usage-guide.md): the canonical tool-selection decision tree for agents.

## Tool Cheat Sheet

| Instead of...                | Use...                           | Saves |
|------------------------------|----------------------------------|-------|
| Read whole file              | `context_bundle(file_path)`      | ~80%  |
| Grep + Read for callers      | `find_callers(name)`             | ~90%  |
| Multiple Read for function   | `find_function(name)`            | ~70%  |
| Manual call graph tracing    | `impact_analysis(name, depth=3)` | ~95%  |
| Grep across codebase         | `search_functions` / `related`   | ~85%  |
| Read file to understand it   | `explore(entity_name)`           | ~75%  |
| Read git log                 | `file_churn(path)` / `hotspots`  | ~70%  |

Read is only for the function body **after** codescope returns the exact file:line.

## Core Architecture

- [crates/core/src/db.rs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/onur-gokyildiz-bhi/codescope/main/crates/core/src/db.rs): unified DB connection layer — remote client over SurrealDB server, one namespace per repo.
- [crates/core/src/graph/schema.rs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/onur-gokyildiz-bhi/codescope/main/crates/core/src/graph/schema.rs): full SurrealQL schema — entity tables (`function`, `file`, `class`, …) + relation tables (`contains`, `calls`, `solves_for`, …).
- [crates/core/src/parser/languages.rs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/onur-gokyildiz-bhi/codescope/main/crates/core/src/parser/languages.rs): tree-sitter language registry. 35+ languages + configs + docs + SQL + Terraform.
- [crates/mcp-server/src/server.rs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/onur-gokyildiz-bhi/codescope/main/crates/mcp-server/src/server.rs): MCP tool surface — 52 tools across search, callgraph, knowledge, conversations, analytics.
- [crates/web/src/lib.rs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/onur-gokyildiz-bhi/codescope/main/crates/web/src/lib.rs): HTTP API for the web UI + MCP HTTP endpoint.

## Knowledge Graph Conventions

- [CLAUDE.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/onur-gokyildiz-bhi/codescope/main/CLAUDE.md): mandatory rules for LLMs using codescope in this repo — search before you build, save significant moments, status tag taxonomy.
- [.claude/rules/codescope-mandatory.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/onur-gokyildiz-bhi/codescope/main/.claude/rules/codescope-mandatory.md): auto-enforcement rules — BEFORE every action, what tool to call.

## API Reference

- [llms-full.txt](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/onur-gokyildiz-bhi/codescope/main/docs/llms-full.txt): every MCP tool with parameters and return shape.

## Companion Tools

- [GSD](https://github.com/gsd-build/get-shit-done): complementary spec-driven workflow. Planning layer (roadmap → phase → plan → execute → verify → ship). GSD subagents auto-use codescope MCP tools when both installed — see [`docs/integrations/gsd.md`](./integrations/gsd.md).
- [context-mode](https://github.com/mksglu/context-mode): complementary generic-tool sandbox + FTS5 retrieval. Any-tool layer. ~98% reduction on large outputs.
- [RTK](https://github.com/rtk-ai/rtk): complementary CLI-output compressor. Shell layer (`git`, `ls`, `cargo`). 60–90% savings on common dev commands.
- Install all four — workflow / code-semantics / tool-output / shell — for the four-layer context diet.

## Optional

- [docs/architecture/REFACTOR-R1-R6.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/onur-gokyildiz-bhi/codescope/main/docs/architecture/REFACTOR-R1-R6.md): post-R1-v2 architecture — bundled surreal server + remote WS clients, why the lock problem went away.
- [crates/e2e/](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/onur-gokyildiz-bhi/codescope/main/crates/e2e/): end-to-end smoke tests — server + CLI + multi-project isolation.
